feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: CIC Support Webcasts
entry.content:
Discover more about CIC support webcasts 

Getting the maximum support from SAP has never been easier! Read on to learn more about the support webcasts on offer by CIC

What are CIC support webcasts? 

CIC support webcasts, which are available to all SAP customers and partners, highlight the key support resources available to you and teach you how to interact successfully with our support organisation. 

If you are unsure of anything during or after the webcast our CIC experts will be on hand to answer any questions you may have via the webcast chat or the Q&A session at the end.  

 

Why sign up for these Support Webcasts? 

By doing so you will become a Po at accessing the key support resources available to you as they teach you how to get the most from your support.  

The support webcasts are offered in a variety of languages such as English, French, Spanish and German, right across different time zones, so there will always be one that is available to suit your needs 

So, what are you waiting for ……………… 

 

Visit the SAP Support Webcast page to REGISTER for one of the next upcoming webcasts! 

Don’t have time to attend one of the live webcast sessions? 

Don’t worry as you can watch one of the recorded sessions by clicking here 

 

A sneak peek of some of the CIC support webcasts available. 

Case Management 

Description: 

Join this support webcast to learn more about the Get Support application including the steps to report and manage your case.  

Our team will walk you through the elements to help you improve the quality of the case being created and generally enhance your support experience with SAP. The aim is that you will understand the SAP processes that you are expected to follow to get the best results from your SAP engagement.   

Learning Objectives:

Best practices for case creation and enabling you on how to navigate and get the best support option from the Get Support Application 

 

Getting the Most From Your Support 

Description: 

In this webinar you will learn about the Support Portal Resources that are available to SAP Customers.  

Learning objective: 

The objectives are to provide the information needed to successfully engage with our support Organization. 

 

Solution Search and Self-Service Enablement 

Description:

In this session you will learn how to search for known issues and resolutions in all of SAP's knowledge repositories. 

Learning Objectives: 

Become more familiar with SAP Support's self-enablement tools and the different ways to find a solution for your issue before a case is needed. 

 

Managing your SAP Universal ID 

Description:

Join this support webcast to learn more about SAP Universal Id, how to create and manage your profile and much more. Our team will walk you through the creation process as well as linking accounts to your profile. We will also cover some common scenarios that customer encounter, to equip you with information in case you face the same issues.  

Learning Objectives: 

Best practices for SAP Universal ID management, enabling you to manage your UID including on how to create, unlinking a user ID, handling email address changes, and more. But that's not all – we understand that recent changes to receiving and transferring certifications on the SAP Training Portal and SAP Learning Hub may have left you with questions. That's why we'll also provide guidance on these changes, ensuring that you have all the information you need to navigate these updates confidently. 

 

Thanks for reading and we look forward to seeing you in our upcoming webcasts!  

   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.   

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community   

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: 🚀 Welcome to the SAP Community Group – Pune! 🚀
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enthusiasts,

I’m Punith Oswal, one of the facilitators of SAP Community Group for Pune, and it gives me immense pleasure to welcome each one of you to this vibrant and thriving community. Whether you're a seasoned professional, a student, or someone just starting your SAP journey, this group is designed to be a collaborative space where we can all grow together.

### Why Join?
The SAP ecosystem is evolving rapidly, and Pune has emerged as a hub for innovation, development, and knowledge-sharing in SAP technologies. This community is a place for all of us to exchange ideas, discuss the latest trends, and network with like-minded individuals who are as passionate about SAP as you are!

### What to Expect:
- Knowledge Sharing: We’ll host regular discussions on SAP-related topics—be it integration (SAP CPI), SAP S/4HANA, analytics, cloud solutions, or under the SAP umbrella.

- Events & Webinars: Look forward to insightful webinars, workshops, and local meetups, where we can dive deep into technical sessions, case studies, and new SAP releases. Don't forget to mark your calendar for special events like *The Future of Integration 2024* happening soon!

- Networking Opportunities: This is a fantastic platform to connect with peers, SAP experts, and industry leaders within the Pune region. You can share your experiences, ask questions, share knowledge and build lasting professional relationships.

- Career Growth: Learn from experts, gain insights into different SAP modules, and enhance your career by staying updated with the latest SAP certifications, projects, and events in Pune.

### How to Participate:
- Ask Questions: No question is too small or too complex! If you're working on a tricky issue or have queries, feel free to post. Someone here will have an answer!

- Share Experiences: We want to hear from you! Whether it's a new project, success story, or challenge you're facing, sharing can help others and foster mutual growth.

- Be Engaged: Participate in polls, join discussions, attend events, and offer your insights. This community thrives when everyone contributes.

Let’s come together to build a strong, dynamic, and inclusive SAP community that empowers every member to succeed. Feel free to introduce yourself in the comments below, and let's get the conversations rolling!

I am also joined by my fellow Pune community members @saicharank @Devkambli @Pari1 @Asutosh21 who are co-running this group with me and helping organize these exciting events. Together, we aim to create an environment where everyone can learn, share, and grow. 

Here’s to learning, growing, and leading together! 🌟

Welcome aboard, and let’s make this journey unforgettable!

 

Warm regards,
Punith Oswal
SAP Community Group - Pune

entry.title: SAP TechEd Virtual & TechEd on Tour (SAUG-x)
entry.content:
SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 is scheduled for Oct 8 & 9. The session catalog has been released and its time to build your agenda. This is available for all and a fantastic opportunity to learn new things.

Check out the session catalog https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

For those who are planning to be at Melbourne, SAUG & SAP are partnering to present an in-person experience for the Developer community. This very special gathering in Melbourne full of unique sessions for in-depth learning, hands-on labs to develop practical skills and deep insights to give you an edge. Draft agenda is out. https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024

See you there.

entry.title: 🌟 Bem-vindos à nossa Comunidade SAP em Curitiba!
entry.content:
Sejam Bem-vindos a nossa comunidade SAP em Curitiba!

Meu nome é Luiz Carlos de Souza, sou de São Paulo porém resido em Curitiba desde 2021, viemos para passar uma temporada em família e nunca mais retornamos, esta cidade é incrível !

Consultor SAP desde 2004, comecei como desenvolvedor ABAP, migrei em 2013 para a área funcional de seguros e atualmente me encontro focado em Arquitetura Cloud com o Business Technology Platform (BTP) para acompanhar toda essa transformação digital. 

Como curador deste grupo, meu objetivo é unir a nossa comunidade local, compartilhando novidades sobre as últimas soluções SAP, promovendo conexões profissionais através de eventuais encontros, e gerando assim um ótimo networking! 

Estou muito animado em começar essa jornada com vocês, e a sua presença é fundamental para construirmos uma comunidade forte e unida. E quem sabe um dia conseguimos ter no nosso próprio SAP Inside Track. (chega de pegar a BR para ir a São Paulo rs....)   

Convido você a deixar um comentário falando um pouco sobre você, sua experiência e o bairro em que mora, ficarei honrado em te conhecer!

Abraços!

entry.title: 🎙️Episódio 02 - Live Track: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito
entry.content:
Neste episódio 2 do Live Track podcast tivemos a honra de entrevistar @Jonys_Arcanjo , consultor SAP SAC/DI do Grupo Aço Cearense. Jonys apresentou uma palestra inspiradora no SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife, abordando como a Inteligência Artificial, em conjunto com o SAP Data Intelligence, está revolucionando a gestão de crédito no setor industrial. Durante o podcast, ele compartilhou insights valiosos sobre a aplicação de IA para prever riscos, otimizar processos e fortalecer a tomada de decisões nas empresas.

🔗🎙️Live Track Podcast: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito

A conversa foi mediada por um time de peso: @Marssel700 @MRobalinho @afigueredo54. Além de explorar os pontos centrais da palestra, discutimos com Jonys como a tecnologia de ponta está moldando o futuro da indústria e o papel do SAP nesse contexto. A troca de ideias foi enriquecida pelas experiências práticas de Jonys, que destacou o impacto direto dessas inovações no dia a dia de grandes empresas como o Grupo Aço Cearense.

Não deixe de conferir essa entrevista imperdível e acompanhar todos os episódios do nosso podcast! E se quiser saber mais sobre as palestras e os eventos do SAP Inside Track 2024, acesse os links abaixo para se manter atualizado com tudo o que está rolando.

Reveja o Episódio 1

Se você gostou do conteúdo, aproveite para seguir o canal e ficar por dentro dos próximos lançamentos e eventos!

A agenda completa do evento já foi divulgada.

Te vejo lá!

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 20th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 23rd.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the first week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  We are kicking things off there with the ever-popular Petoberfest. 

List of activities for contest week 1: 

DAYBADGE CODECONTENT WEEK POINTS
 Devtoberfest 2024 ParticipantRegister200
Monday#5E1C26Devtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 1, Sept. 231200
Monday#D2EAF1Learn about the ABAP Data Dictionary150
Monday#BA7CC8Create an ABAP Database Table and Relevant ABAP Dictionary Objects750
Monday#1B0B12Create Database Table and Generate UI Service200
Monday#725772Devtoberfest 2024 - CAP, Week 1, Sept. 231200
Monday#94918CCreate a CAP Business Service with Node.js Using Visual Studio Code500
Monday#FAC0AADeploy a CAP Business Service to SAP Business Technology Platform300
Tuesday#2B2924Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Week 1, Sept. 241200
Tuesday#D88A4DSAP Crossword1800
Tuesday#A5E5FBCreate a Composite Component (List with Rating)200
Tuesday#083676Create a Social Media Backend150
Tuesday#111D2ACreate a Product List Page with Ratings150
Tuesday#433433Prepare a Product Page150
Tuesday#2A170ACreate the Product Page UI with Ratings/Comments400
Wednesday#FEFEFFDevtoberfest 2024 - Integration, Week 1, Sept. 251200
Wednesday#676F49Set Up Prerequisites for your SAP Integration Suite & SAP Build Apps Mission200
Wednesday#5D219AConsume a Stripe service from SAP Open Connectors and SAP Cloud Integration to create payment transactions100
Wednesday#464471Save, Filter and Get your needed data in your Integration Flow100
Wednesday#D7D8D8Consume a SAP Sales and Service Core API to create Sales Orders using SAP Cloud Integration300
Wednesday#369A33Send SMS using SAP Cloud Integration while consuming a Twilio API100
Wednesday#B4ABA9Configure SAP API Management policies to avoid CORS issues and generate an API Key200
Thursday#EDBABCDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Week 1 - Sep. 261200
Thursday#190B04Use Trial to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application50
Thursday#413E44Use Trial to Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and Document Information Extraction250
Friday#85A969Devtoberfest 2024 - Frontend, Week 1 - Sep. 271200
Friday#228E80Get Ready for UI5-Development on Your Local Machine50
Friday#8F5D85Create an SAPUI5 App from the Command Line150
Friday Devtoberfest 2024 - Petoberfest1000

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Germany 2024-09 Recap
entry.content:
In mid-September, we held two SAP CodeJam events in Germany: in Isernhagen and Stuttgart. The events focused on getting started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python.

The first one was kindly hosted by Inwerken, and it was great to catch up with @EnnoWulff, who took this picture of me below...

...inviting participants to join our free online Devtoberfest event starting on September 23: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-welcome/ba-p/9358

Then on the way to Stuttgart I had a chance to stop by our SAP headquarters in Walldorf...

...where I had a chance to meet with @dinah for the first time in-real-life...

...and to play with some cool technology.

The second event was kindly hosted by Bosch Digital in Stuttgart and I would like to thank Gamze Öztürk and @AjayKulkarni for the effort to organize it!

And it is always a pleasure to catch up with @cyclingfisch_ with whom we had our micro SAP Stammtisch 🍻

Now, the next stops are in Lisbon on September 25th hosted by SAP Portugal: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-lisboa-pt/ev-p/13773843 and in Porto on September 27 hosted by SAP Inside Track Porto: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-porto-pt/ev-p/13770170 

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-quot-as-a-new-sap/ba-p/13574098

entry.title: 𝐒𝐀𝐏 𝐈𝐧𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐞 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐆𝐮𝐫𝐮𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 2024: A Remarkable Experience
entry.content:
The morning of SAP Inside Track, Gurugram

As one of the core team members of the much-anticipated SAP Inside Track, Gurugram 2024 event, I woke up to the sound of rainfall still pattering against my balcony. The city was already drenched with heavy rainfall the previous day, and the forecast wasn’t looking any better for today either. My heart raced with a mix of anxiety and excitement. Would the weather dampen the spirits of our attendees? Would all our meticulous planning go in vain? Despite these worries, I knew the SIT event would be a success. As I arrived at the venue, I met the volunteers along with my fellow core team members Vipul Khullar, Vartika Gupta, Anuradha Dhingan, Meghna Patel and Neeraj Manocha. The energy among us was palpable, and as the first guests started to trickle in, we felt a surge of optimism. And there we knew it was going to be a day to remember!

 

 

Kick-off         

The event kicked off with an inspiring welcome note by Aseem Khera, Location Head at SAP Labs India, Gurugram. He underscored the pivotal role of the SAP community in driving innovation and fostering collaboration, emphasizing how events like these create invaluable synergies. Aseem also highlighted SAP’s strategic focus on leveraging artificial intelligence to deliver enhanced value to its customers.

 

This was followed by an inspiring video keynote delivered by Sindhu Gangadharan, Managing Director of SAP Labs India, Head of Customer Innovation Services, and Chairperson of NASSCOM. She emphasized the significance of learning and networking through community events such as SAP Inside Track. Sindhu discussed the substantial impact of artificial intelligence on the global economy and highlighted SAP's pivotal role in assisting businesses to harness the extensive economic advantages of it. Furthermore, she encouraged community members to aspire to become SAP Champions and SAP Mentors.

 

This was succeeded by a keynote presentation titled “Identifying Value-Based Business AI Scenarios with a Human-Centric Approach,” delivered by Kamalika Bhar of our sponsor Mindset Consulting and Divya Chandrika Mohan from SAP.

 

The morning track was successfully concluded with a presentation on SAP Business AI by Sudhir Verma, the Head of Cross Technology & Architecture for Private Cloud Products at SAP. During this session, Sudhir offered valuable insights into the transformative potential of Generative AI, highlighting key innovations such as Joule and the GenAI Hub. Additionally, Neha Anand from SAP showcased various productive use cases utilizing Joule, further enriching the discussion.

 

I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to our host, Vartika Gupta, for expertly keeping the energy and momentum alive!

 

Following, track 1, several sessions were conducted throughout the day, concentrating on SAP Generative AI, SAP Business AI, and a variety of practical use cases across Tracks 2 to 4. Presenters from SAP Labs India, Mindset, IBM, and EY provided valuable insights into how Generative AI within SAP applications is enhancing value for both businesses and customers.

 

Engaging Sessions: Learning and Networking

Track 2

  1. Unleashing the power of Retrieval Augmented Generation with SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub and SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

     Speaker: Deep Srivastava

 

 

2. SAP Gen AI solutions that won hearts in SAP Hack2Build 2024

   Speaker: Aayush Dhawan

 

3. SAP AI Business Services - Al models, pre-trained on business relevant data

Speaker: Anuradha Dhingan

 

 4. Building an Application with SAP Build Code and Joule

Speaker: Ravi Grover

 

5. SAP Business AI for A Better Gurugram

Speaker: Niladri Bihari Nayak & Haritosh Srivastav

 

Track 3

1. Umbrella of SAP BTP AI

Speaker:  Chandra Bhushan Singh & Mayank Singhal

 

2. Building a Data fabric and Unlocking Business Value with AI in SAP Datasphere

Speaker: Akhil Das & Pratik Doshi

 

3. From Insights to Experience with Business AI in SAP Digital Supply Chain

Speaker: Raunak Agarwal & Shreyance Parakh

 

4. CX AI Toolkit

Speaker: Aditi Dahal, Nikkita & Siddharth Agarwal

 

5. Empower Your Business with Intelligent Situation Handling and Joule

Speaker: Ranjeet Kumar

 

6. SAP Business AI - Innovations in Procurement & Supply Chain

Speaker: Rakesh Pupneja

 

Track 4 was dedicated to Hands on experience activities:

Participants had the opportunity to explore SAP's newest AI features and capabilities through hands-on experience. During the interactive sessions, participants created close to 50 SAP-based applications, showcasing how AI can be practically applied in various business scenarios.

 

Sponsors

Thanks to our esteemed sponsors Deloitte and Mindset and for their contribution in making SAP Inside Track Gurugram 2024 - Business AI Edition a memorable event. 

A Day to Remember: Summary and Reflections

In conclusion, the SAP Inside Track (SIT) was not merely an event; it represented a convergence of shared knowledge, networking opportunities, and collaborative learning within the SAP Community. Over 100 plus community members participated, providing valuable constructive feedback on the overall experience.

 

Throughout the day, I had the privilege of meeting remarkable individuals, and I eagerly anticipate the upcoming SAP Inside Tracks in Gurugram to reconnect with them. The energy and enthusiasm were palpable, a testament to the collective passion driving us forward. I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to all the fellow organizers, volunteers, speakers, and our sponsors, as their contributions were instrumental in the event's success. Thank you once again for your exceptional efforts and commitment. I look forward to collaborating with you all on future endeavours.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: Addressing UNSDGs Through Women-Centric Education
entry.content:
As Global Goals Week kicks off at the United Nations Headquarters in New York, Barefoot College International (BCI) reflects on their pledge to address the Sustainability Development Goals (SDGs). Their vocational and livelihood programs holistically offer solutions to 15 of the 17 goals. In the spirit of “leaving no one behind,” BCI focuses their programmatic responses on the accessibility of education and resources for marginalized women.

Overall, it’s because of this women-centric approachthat 15 of the 17 goals are naturally addressed; women and girls of the Global South are often the last to receive educational and entrepreneurial resources. Thus, uplifting women accelerates global prosperity, peace, and Climate Justice, which are key components of the global goals.

SDG #1: No Poverty

Communities and societies are only as strong as their weakest link. Women, the most untapped resource in impoverished villages, have great potential when included in the workforce. Women are statistically responsible for more than three times as much domestic unpaid labor within the family unit. Offering women education, leading to better entrepreneurial and livelihood options locally, increases household incomes, resulting in a higher quality of life.

SDG #4: Quality Education

Offering women and girls educational resources not only provides them with chances at better-paid income jobs, but it also imparts valuable information on their health, including sexual health, planned parenthood, women’s rights, and nutrition. This increases living standards, improves sexual health, lowers the birth rate, and assists women with knowing their legal rights.

SDG #5: Gender Equality

Gender inequality stagnates social progress, due to the loss of potential workforce professionals when women are not included. BCI’s primary mission is to create lasting change from the ground up through harnessing the power of rural communities and their women. This includes ensuring that the next generation is motivated to keep girl children in school, creating a ripple effect of systemic change toward gender-balanced development.

SDG #10: Reduced Inequalities

When we tie poverty to gender inequality, focusing on the education of women is automatically a means to foster a more equitable playing field for rural communities. Often lagging behind their urban neighbors, remote regions have had an uphill battle in maintaining healthy literacy rates and digital savviness. This can be attributed to a lack of accessibility, thus offering these regions the tools to gain experience and knowledge helps them catch up and thrive.

SDG #17: Partnerships for the Goals

BCI has recognized the value of meaningful, lucrative partnerships with entities whose missions align with theirs. To achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UNSDGs) in a timely manner globally, partnering with like-minded organizations is essential.

Call to action

To join forces with BCI, or to support their ongoing work focused on women, education, and sustainability, please visit their website, www.barefootcollege.org.

Also, be sure to check out the latest research from the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team and learn more about SAP’s “zero emissions, zero waste, and zero inequality” approach to sustainability.

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Pune 2024
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the speaker registration for SAP Inside Track Pune 2024, happening on October 19th, 2024! Our focus is on the transformative power of SAP Business AI, and we are looking for passionate speakers to share their expertise and insights across various tracks. If you have experience in building AI applications, creating innovative Business AI solutions, or working with Embedded AI in SAP products, we invite you to contribute to our event.
 
Event Details:
Date: October 19th, 2024
Time: 9 AM - 5 PM IST
Venue: SAP Labs India, ITPP, Pune
 
Find more about the event in the announcement blog post: SAP Inside Track Pune 2024 - Announcement 
 
Speaker Tracks for your proposal:
Track 1: Building AI Applications on SAP BTP
Dive into the technicalities of building AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Demonstrate how developers and architects can leverage SAP BTP to create robust AI solutions that drive business value.

Track 2: Business AI Solutions from Ecosystem - Partners & Customers
Showcase successful AI solutions developed by SAP partners and customers. Share case studies, implementation experiences, and collaborative projects that highlight the innovative use of Business AI within the SAP ecosystem.

Track 3: Embedded AI across Solutions - SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products
Explore the integration of Embedded AI in various SAP products like SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, CX, and more. Discuss how these intelligent features are transforming business processes and enhancing user experiences.
 
Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Pune 2024?
  • Showcase Your Expertise: Share your knowledge with a passionate and engaged audience of SAP professionals.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with fellow experts, partners, and SAP enthusiasts.
  • Gain Recognition: Enhance your professional profile and gain recognition within the SAP community
How to Register:
Ready to share your insights at SAP Inside Track Pune 2024? Register now to become a speaker and contribute to one of the most exciting SAP community events of the year.

Speaker Registration Link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScrOZES6KJUk279dl06X4hzaQQMRuLeHkTGrX7wloUnXXpCcQ/viewform
 
Next Steps: Once you submit your proposal,
Step 1: Our expert team will go through your proposal and if it is shortlisted, we will contact your for more details.
Step 2: This will again be discussed internally with the experts and once shortlisted, we will inform you about the next steps – like presentation format, social media advertisement etc.,
We look forward to your valuable contributions and to seeing you at SAP Inside Track Pune 2024!
 
 
Best Regards,
SAP Community Pune Core Team

entry.title: Devtoberfest has started
entry.content:
Hi Everyone 🙂

Devtoberfest has started this week. It will last for 5 weeks – with nearly 70 sessions, contest, tutorials and more...

Here is the general overview: Devtoberfest overview 

You can find the events schedule here: Devtoberfest-events 

Also there is the challenging contest, where you can earn points and Community badges. Here is the links to the contest overview and the first weeks activities: Contest overview ;  Devtoberfest Contest - first week 

And the fun part of course - Petoberfest. 

Sooo, good luck and have fun 🎉

entry.title: Navigating the Complexities of Modern Data Integration
entry.content:
Navigating the Complexities of Modern Data Integration

In today's digital landscape, organizations face a multitude of challenges due to distributed data assets, integration hurdles, and data sprawl. As data integration becomes increasingly complex, it is essential to understand the key capabilities required to effectively manage this task. This blog post highlights important data integration use cases and provides an overview of the solutions offered by SAP to help businesses streamline their data management efforts.

Key Use Cases in Data Integration

  1. Data Replication: This involves the integration of data through various methodologies such as extract, transform, load (ETL) and extract, load, transform (ELT). A common scenario is replicating data from legacy systems into SAP HANA for analytics and migration.

  2. Data Virtualization: This approach allows the creation of a virtual data layer by federating queries across remote data sources without the need for physical data movement. An example is accessing shared master data across multiple sources for seamless virtual access.

  3. Data Quality Management (DQM): DQM focuses on integrating data from different sources for cleansing, matching, and consolidation. This can include address data cleansing or embedding validation services within applications to ensure data quality.

  4. Data Orchestration: This entails pipeline-driven integration of disparate data types, supporting distributed processing. It plays a vital role in enabling data science and machine learning initiatives by integrating data from various enterprise assets.

Solutions for Enterprise Information and Data Management

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of solutions that simplify integration, allowing organizations to derive maximum value from their data landscapes while minimizing effort. These solutions encompass capabilities essential for sophisticated data operations, including data integration, metadata management, and data processing.

Data Integration: Achieving high-quality data access and movement is critical for effective system performance. While ETL methodologies have historically been the norm, SAP offers a broad range of connectivity options to accommodate diverse on-premise, cloud, and hybrid systems. This includes various adapters and connectors designed to facilitate deep integration with SAP’s business software.

Metadata Management: Organizations generate vast amounts of data in different formats, necessitating a robust understanding of this information. SAP’s metadata management capabilities enable users to catalog data effectively and assess its quality. This centralization helps identify data improvement needs, ensuring that only trustworthy data is utilized for business operations.

Data Processing: With ever-increasing data volumes, point-to-point replication is often insufficient. SAP's data processing capabilities allow for scalable solutions that meet workload demands. This includes remote orchestration and the ability to push processing to where data resides, optimizing performance and resource usage.

SAP Products and Solutions

SAP's portfolio includes various products designed to meet diverse data management needs:

  • SAP Landscape Transformation Replication Server: Facilitates real-time data replication from ABAP and non-ABAP systems into SAP HANA and other environments.

  • SAP Information Steward: Provides a unified platform for data quality monitoring and improvement, catering to data stewards and IT professionals.

  • SAP HANA Smart Data Integration: Offers real-time data replication and transformation capabilities, alongside smart data access for remote data usage without physical copying.

  • SAP Data Services: Empowers users to integrate and transform data efficiently, featuring a wide array of connectors for third-party applications.

  • SAP Data Intelligence: A comprehensive solution for managing data in multi-cloud and hybrid environments, enabling governance, integration, processing, and orchestration of data across various landscapes.

These products are specifically optimized for targeted use cases, delivering robust services tailored to individual organizational needs.

Conclusion

As organizations continue to navigate the complexities of modern data landscapes, leveraging the right tools and solutions is paramount for effective data integration. SAP's comprehensive offerings enable businesses to streamline their data management processes, ensuring they can meet evolving business requirements and drive meaningful insights from their data assets.

entry.title: How to unlock Team Potential with Organization and Relationship Systems Coaching?
entry.content:
In ORSC we distinguish three interconnected forms of intelligence that are important when we engage in groups:

  • Emotional Intelligence describes the relationship with ourselves and our capacity to articulate and respond to our personal emotions.
    – How do I dance?

  • Social Intelligence describes our relationship with others and how we can empathize with them.
    – What is it like to dance together?

  • Relationship Systems Intelligence describes the relationships across the team, group, or system and what emerges from the collective.
    – What is it in the dance that emerges among us?

Relationship Systems Intelligence

Principles of Relationship Systems Intelligence

The following principles of Relationship Systems Intelligence are the foundation of the ORSC framework which does not focus on individuals but on the system like e.g. a team that they are part of:

  • Each relationship system has its own unique identity.
    – If you were to describe the team as a person, what characteristics would it have?

  • Each member of the relationship system is a voice of the system.
    – What perspectives within the team haven't been fully heard?

  • Relationship systems are inherently intelligent, generative and creative.
    – What patterns and shifts do you experience in the team?

  • Relationship systems rely on roles to organize and perform functions.
    – How are the different roles serving the team as a whole?

  • Relationship systems are in a constant state of emergence.
    – What are ways we can embrace changes to foster a continuous development of the team?

 

Meta-Skills for Coaching Relationship Systems

Coaching always has a being and a doing aspect. The best intervention is not effective if the atmosphere is not right. Thus, ORSC coaches also focus on the “come from” place from which the work is done and create intentional emotional fields using the following meta-skills:

  • The Heart aspect focuses on building strong, authentic relationships within a team to create a foundation of trust and empathy.
    – What actions can you take to deepen your relationships and build more trust and empathy within your team?

  • Deep Democracy emphasizes the importance of hearing all voices and perspectives within the system, ensuring inclusive decision-making and valuing diverse viewpoints.
    – Which perspective might be missing, and how can we make sure everyone feels heard?

  • Playfulness encourages a light-hearted and curious approach to challenges encouraging open exploration.
    – How would a magician solve the problem?

  • Respect is honouring each team member’s unique contributions, fostering an environment of mutual appreciation.
    – What do you appreciate about each team members contributions?

  • Collaboration and Partnership are valuing each team member's strengths and promoting cooperative efforts toward shared goals.
    – How can we leverage the strengths of each team member?

  • Enquiry and Awareness encourage curiosity to peel the onion of what is happening.
    – What is happening now? And now?

  • Commitment is required to take over responsibility, and follow-through in achieving a team’s objectives.
    – What specific commitments are we willing to make to ensure we achieve our shared objectives?

  • The Other aspect fosters an understanding that a team is part of a larger systems that impact its functioning.
    – What external factors might be influencing your team, and how should you consider these in your strategy?

 

Dimensions of Relationship Systems

The three dimensions in the middle of the ORSC model indicate how a relationship system expresses itself:

  • The Intelligence dimension recognizes that every system has an innate ability to self-organize, solve problems, and generate innovative solutions. An ORSC coach can support a team to access its own collective intelligence to live its full potential.
    – What is trying to happen?

  • The Geography dimension explores spaces within a relationship system that shapes its interactions. An ORSC coach can help a team map out these different territories to better understand how they affect communication and collaboration.
    – Which perspectives within the team are left out?

  • The Path dimension focuses on the journey of the relationship system over time. An ORSC coach can enable a team to explore past successes and challenges, to clarify current goals and values, and to envision a shared future.
    – What would success look like for your team and how will we know you have arrived at this future?

 

Workshop Insights

As an ORSC coach, my role is not to fix or repair a system, but rather to illuminate the inherent dynamics, strengths, and patterns already present within it. By facilitating a space where the system's natural tendencies and underlying structures are revealed, a shared experience is created that brings awareness to hidden potential and unseen opportunities for growth.

As a client of a team workshop I recently held sums it up: "Thanks to you, we shared all together a precious moment, full of emotion, energy and fun. You offered a safe space. You gently guided us to connect with ourselves and express our creativity. You helped us listen to all voices, realize our strengths, reflect on our needs and build on our commonalities and our experience. In our daily lives, we tend to forget them. It's good to remember that we have a solid foundation on which to build."

 

Originally published on my blog imagium.de

entry.title: Level Complete! SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 Recap
entry.content:
Another fantastic SAP Inside Track Belgium is in the books! Last Saturday, we came together to share knowledge, connect with passionate SAP Community members, and celebrate everything that makes this community so special.

 

A Day of Learning and Connecting

The day was packed with insightful sessions from talented speakers who shared their expertise on a wide range of SAP topics. It’s always inspiring to see how much we can learn from each other and how strong the spirit of collaboration is within the SAP ecosystem. A huge thank you to all our speakers for their time and contributions.

 

Shoutout to Our Host

This year, we had the privilege of hosting the event at the ARHS Group's fantastic venue. Their support and hospitality truly elevated the experience for everyone involved, and we couldn’t have asked for a better partner to make SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 a success!
 

 

A Pre-Event to Remember

Before the main event, a group of us had the chance to experience a brilliant pre-event Brussels City tour, expertly guided by Jeremy. While Belgium may be famous for its beer, Jeremy showed us there’s so much more to discover! From the stunning architecture to rich cultural spots, it was the perfect way to kick off the weekend. Thanks, Jeremy!

 

What’s Next?

As we wrap up another successful SAP Inside Track Belgium, we’re already looking ahead to the next SAP Community Meetup in Belgium. Stay tuned for more details—we can’t wait to do it all over again with you!

entry.title: We Value Your Feedback: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 was an incredible day, and we’re so grateful for all of you who joined us! To ensure future events continue to meet the needs and expectations of our community, we asked for your feedback using Mentimeter—and the results are in!
 

What You Told Us

Here’s what we learned from your input:
 
- One or two tracks? The overwhelming majority of you prefer a single track to keep the focus streamlined and allow everyone to attend all sessions.
  

 

- Hands-on sessions or not? The answer was a resounding yes! Expect more hands-on, interactive opportunities in future events to dive deeper into SAP technologies.

 

  
- Full day or half day? Well, I admit, the half-day suggestion might have been a bit of a stretch. Everyone agreed a full day is the way to go for maximum value (lesson learned!).

 

- How many SAP Inside Tracks per year? Most of the community feels that one event per year is just right to balance time, content, and planning.

 

Help Us Choose the Next Location!

While we’re thrilled with the feedback, we still have one big question: Where should the next SAP Inside Track Belgium be held? We're considering several cities like Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent, or Liège, but we’d love to hear your thoughts! Is there another city you think would be perfect for the event? Let us know in the comments below.
 
Your voice continues to shape the future of SAP Inside Track Belgium. Thanks again for your feedback, and we can’t wait to see you at the next event!

entry.title: Parallel Accounting and Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S4 HANA Finance
entry.content:
Parallel Accounting and Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S4 HANA Finance

Vol. 1 Parallel Accounting

What is Parallel Accounting?

There are different accounting standards that ensure accuracy and transparency for the organization. It allows fundamental frameworks, policies, and rules to report the financial transaction. 

While business operates into a broader scope in terms of geopolitical phenomena, it requires or has an organizational mandate to follow compliance in the global as well as regional environment in terms of attending accountability and reporting transparency. Moreover, legal compliance creates a crucial role to accord with more than one accounting standard. Here parallel accounting comes into play to comply with transparency, regional competency, and accountability into the reporting standard.

Parallel accounting is one of the crucial subsets of Universal Journal that allows multidimensional reporting and allows integrated financial data reporting in a single chart of accounts.

Parallel accounting enables maintaining multiple accounting standards, like US GAAP and IFRS, based on business requirements and compliance mandates. It allows organizations to propose the financial report on different accounting standards and financial statement versions that cater to different regulatory requirements, industry standards, and internal reporting needs.

Why Parallel Accounting?

Financial reporting is crucial to organizational transparency and accountability. Moreover, it reflects financial performances to the external and internal stakeholders. Hence, maintaining relevant accounting standards for financial reports is crucial to ensuring financial accountability across the globe.

Now it is very important to accommodate or enable parallel accounting into SAP that complies with complex characteristics of financial components. Let’s take an example of incurring research and development expenses for innovation. According to the US GAAP, research and development expenses are treated as operating expenses that would reflect into the P/L statement as expenses. On the other hand, IFRS considers research and development expenses as assets that would come under the asset side on the balance sheet. Now the question is how to report the financial statement for “Research and Development,” while organizations need to comply with both US GAAP and IFRS. Here, parallel accounting enables the solution for the above question. It designed the functionality of the recording; the transaction has different characteristics based upon different accounting standards.

Scope of Parallel Accounting

The scope of parallel accounting is wide and has been applied to the different application components. Below are the key application components where parallel accounting plays a crucial role.

  1. · General Ledger Accounting

Based on the business requirement, parallel accounting allows regrouping and sorting of receivables and payables for different accounting principles. Parallel accounting also enables dynamic value adjustments for doubtful receivables. Moreover, parallel accounting intervenes in foreign currency valuation and allows posting for adjusted values of transactions posted in foreign currency.

  1. · Asset accounting

SAP allows to define different depreciation areas based on the different accounting principles. By assigning types of valuation or accounting principles to depreciation areas, SAP allows the integration between parallel valuations based on different accounting principles in asset accounting. There are two different ways to integrate asset accounting with parallel valuation; one is the ledger approach, and the other is the account approach. In the ledger approach, accounting principles are assigned to separate ledgers, which allows for the posting of separate documents for each accounting principle or valuation. In the account approach, different accounts are being created that represent different valuations for asset accounting. Likewise, in the ledger approach, separate documents are posted for each accounting principle or valuation.

  1. · Treasury and Risk Management

There is a strong integration of parallel accounting with Treasury and risk management. In Treasury and Risk Management, systems allow to post accounting entries in different valuation areas in accordance with different accounting principles. Here, valuation areas portray distinct accounting principles. Valuation areas are assigned with accounting principles and allow to post values to different accounting principles.

Valuation areas offer different classification categories that can be used for valuation specifications for each accounting principle. Below are the classification categories used in Treasury and Risk Management.

Valuation classes allow to distinguish financial assets as per the holding categories, such as availability for sales or held-to-maturity for the IFRS statement. As assets are assigned to different valuation classes, financial transactions can be posted on the specific accounts based on the nature and characteristics of the transaction.

Differentiation helps to define the structure of the balance sheet accounts by using characteristics upon them.

Single-position management proposes the concept of lot accounting based on which different consumption sequence procedures can be applied for specific financial products.

Position management procedures enable control over balance sheet accounts with three key parameters: valuation classes, valuation areas, and other characteristics.

  1. · Parallel Accounting in Controlling

In Controlling, multiple valuation approaches can be displayed in accordance with different valuation accounting principles used in the business vertical. The system enables features to use parallel accounting by using additional accounts. Based on the present scenarios, businesses will decide the accounting principles used for controlling and, moreover, need to create cost elements and revenue elements for accounting principles. Position Management Procedures, a feature, enable prescribed valuation approaches that can be assigned to balance sheet accounts based on valuation areas, valuation class, and other relevant characteristics.

 

 

Benefits of Parallel Accounting

  • Enable multidimensional reporting and integrated financial data processing: Parallel accounting is one of the crucial subsets of Universal Journal that allows multidimensional reporting and allows integrated financial data reporting in a single chart of accounts.
  • Allows different financial statement versions: It allows organizations to propose the financial report on different accounting standards and financial statement versions that cater to different regulatory requirements, industry standards, and internal reporting needs. Moreover,
  • Parallel accounting and currencies: Parallel accounting enables using one local currency and an additional two parallel currencies to represent distinct valuations based on the currency. In a simple word, SAP allows 3 multi-dimensional currencies for each accounting principle. With multi-currency capabilities, businesses can process their valuation and key closing operations.
  • Provides consolidated views and alternative fiscal year variant approaches: Parallel accounting offers increased data transparency and real-time analytics through consolidated views that help in potential decision-making. Moreover, parallel accounting offers alternative fiscal year variant reports that provide comprehensive analytics and insights.
  • Removal of data redundancy: Parallel accounting enables diagnosed, effective, and sustainable reporting procedures that reduce data duplication and redundancy.

Approaches in SAP

There are two ways to maintain parallel accounting in SAP: the accounting approach and the ledger approach.

  1. Accounting Approach:

In the accounting approach, each general ledger account with the distinct characteristics gets created and aligned with responsible financial statement versions. Each chart of accounts is created based on accounting principles, and any number of financial statement versions can be assigned per chart of accounts. So, the account approach ecosystem contains different general ledger accounts, like adjustments accounts with different financial statement versions, and only one ledger. Separate documents are getting posted on each valuation or accounting principle.

Figure 1: Object flows in Accounting Approach

  1. Ledger Approach:

In the ledger approach, a separate ledger is being created in accordance with separate accounting principles (GAAP and IFRS). With this ledger approach, accounting entries should be posted to the specific ledger according to the nature of the transaction. However, accounting entries are getting posted into two ledgers at a single time, whether there are no specifications or indications of ledgers to be used. In the ledger approach ecosystem, there are only single financial versions with different ledgers. However, there is no requirement of creating adjustment accounts.

Figure 2: Object flows in Ledger approach

  1. Leading Ledger

Leading ledger is the default ledger that is assigned to all company codes and acts as a global ledger. In SAP S4 HANA, the leading ledger is referred to as 0L.

  1. Non-Leading Ledger

Non-leading can be referred to as a parallel ledger that represents the local accounting principles being used for reporting purposes. In SAP S4 HANA, non-leading ledger is marked as 2L.

 

Vol 2: Universal Parallel Accounting

 

Release

This concept is introduced in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2105 release, and delivered to SAP S/4HANA 2022 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition customers in October 2022.

Background

Universal Parallel Accounting proposed a harmonized reporting architecture for ledgers and currencies that makes period-end reporting simpler and reduces complexity across the entire company accounting landscape. It enables parallel management valuations represented by additional currency types to distinguish a legal valuation from a group valuation.

There are some key challenges in traditional parallel accounting that lead to additional period-end closing activities, cumbersome filtering of values in reporting, and complex configuration. Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) revokes those challenges and offers a synchronized and harmonized architecture for reporting and analytics. 

Features and benefits of Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA)

UPA has played a pivotal role in group valuation and multi-currency reporting. This section will demonstrate the key features and benefits from UPA that businesses can achieve in terms of smooth business operation and financial reporting. With unconsolidated views, Universal Parallel Accounting allows valuation of material based on different ledger prices and asserts production variances and contribution margins for each ledger. Alternative Fiscal Years allows multi-fiscal year capabilities per ledger and/or company code to overcome the boundaries of fiscal year variant complexities and additional period-end activities. Multicurrency capabilities support up to ten currencies to calculate and post in parallel. Moreover, parallel accounting supports settlement per ledger and introduces the concept of ledger-specific settlement rules. In addition to this, UPA supports group valuation with consolidated views approaches, which eliminate inter-company revenues and COGS.

UPA in inventory valuation

UPA proposes an extensive segmentation approach to inventory valuation in terms of ledger-specific valuation, which allows more granular representation of different accounting standards for standard costs, work-in-process, finished goods inventories, and actual cost of goods sold. UPA introduces the value approach with settlement per ledger, actual costing runs per ledger, and inventory price per ledger. An audit trail for standard and actual costing can be executed with enhanced transparency and accuracy.

UPA in Asset Accounting

Universal parallel accounting enables settlement runs for AuC to final asset per ledger, which leads to monitoring and tracking of the complete asset life cycle picture per ledger basis. It also helps to assert actual asset values per accounting standard at the ledger level.

UPA in Overhead accounting

Previously, in General Ledger Accounting only the values in the leading ledger were transferred to Controlling that leads resulting in balances being left on the project in any additional valuations (ledger 2L, here) and an incorrect value on the receiver in the second ledger. With Universal Parallel Accounting, SAP introduces a pure ledger approach where the two sets of values are handled completely independently and the values on the project are cleared properly and the assets under construction are capitalized correctly in each ledger

\

Conclusion

Parallel Accounting is an enabling capability to perform valuations and closing preparations according to the accounting principles. This key enabler provides a comparative data reporting architecture that can either be based on dedicated ledgers or dedicated general ledger accounts within one ledger.

 

 

SAP ERP add-on for financial accounting and operations SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP Asset Strategy and Performance Management FIN Cost Object Controlling FIN Accounts Receivable and Payable   FIN Controlling FIN Cost Object Controlling SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting FIN Asset Accounting FIN Treasury SAP Treasury and Risk Management Treasury Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting SAP Group Reporting Data Collection SD (Sales and Distribution) FIN Material Ledger MM (Materials Management) SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP Receivables Manager 

@DataTherapist, @Laura Schmitz @Christoph Christophersen

entry.title: Celebrating the Successful Completion of SIT Hyderabad 21st Sep 2024
entry.content:
On September 21st, SIT Hyderabad marked a significant milestone with the successful completion of our event, which brought together over 220 enthusiastic participants. This year’s event was a testament to our commitment to fostering innovation, collaboration, and hands-on learning within the tech community.

Event Highlights

Impressive Turnout

With more than 220 attendees, SIT Hyderabad showcased the growing interest and engagement within our community. The diverse group of participants included industry professionals, tech enthusiasts, and students, all eager to learn, share, and network.

Diverse Tracks and Sessions

This year, we organized the event into four distinct tracks, each designed to cater to different interests and expertise levels

Community and Networking

Beyond the sessions and workshops, SIT Hyderabad 2024 was a platform for networking and community building. Participants had the chance to connect with like-minded individuals, share ideas, and forge new professional relationships. The event fostered a sense of community, encouraging collaboration and continuous learning.

Looking Ahead

The success of SIT Hyderabad 2024 is a testament to the hard work and dedication of our organizers, speakers, and participants. We are grateful for the overwhelming support and enthusiasm from the community. As we look ahead, we are committed to continuing this journey of innovation and learning, and we are excited to bring even more engaging and impactful events in the future.

Thank you to everyone who made SIT Hyderabad 2024 a resounding success. We look forward to seeing you at our next event!

Thanks & Regards
Ruthvik Chowdary

entry.title: B2B Integration Factory's Role and Value Adds in the B2B Cloud Integration journey
entry.content:
Introduction

Following on the B2B Integration Factory - Custom Extensions blog post, I would like to demonstrate how the B2B Integration Factory packages differ from the standard Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2 bundle and how we operate from the backoffice. Further, I also want to emphasize how our B2B Integration Factory service benefit our customers. Lastly, I want to show some features that are already supported by the B2B Integration Factory. 

B2B Integration Factory vs Standard TPM bundle

To summarize it in one sentence: 
B2B Integration Factory cover requirements and provide possible extensions and custom features that are not covered by the Standard yet. Following image shows how we operate and what we envision:

1. Customer asks for a feature request to B2B Integration Factory. It is either possible via SAP Business Accelerator Hub or via mail to b2bintegrationfactory@sap.com
2. B2B Integration Factory collects and prioritizes the requested features. We also analyze, if this feature request can be realized by integration flow extensions. If not, the B2B Integration Factory refers the feature request to the product development (including priority) with the ask for an estimation and delivery date. 
3. Define the details for the feature required and maybe define required custom parameters, which will be used in TPM for the configuration.
4. B2B Integration Factory shows the details of the feature extension to the product development and negotiate on the concepts and custom parameters so that the migration path of the configured data that are required for the feature request are minimal. This will be a contract between the B2B Integration Factory and the product development.
5. Based on the agreements, the B2B Integration Factory informs costumer on the intended concept, when the Factory extension + the standard feature will be delivered.
6. B2B Integration Factory implements the extension into the corresponding B2B Integration Factory packages according the agreements with the product development.
7. B2B Integration Factory publishes a new version of the corresponding B2B Integration Factory packages with new feature at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub à Community Section.
8. B2B Integration Factory informs the customer that new version of the B2B Integration Factory packages with the requested feature is available.
9. Customer downloads the available integration package from the standard or community section accordingly.
10. Product development implements the agreed feature extension into the TPM/IA solution and updates the standard integration package accordingly.
11. B2B Integration Factory adjusts and optimizes the impacted Factory integration flow based on the standard extensions.
12. B2B Integration Factory substitutes the complete corresponding integration flow, if all feature extensions are supported by the standard TPM/IA solution and their corresponding standard integration flow.
13. Product development publishes the updated standard integration package in the Business Accelerator Hub standard section.
14. Customer may substitute the B2B Integration Factory package (provided in the community section) with the standard integration package (provided in the standard section). This can be possible, if all the customer requirements are fulfilled by the standard integration package.

B2B Integration Factory - What are the benefits?

The key assets and value adds of the B2B Integration Factory initiative are:

  1. Fulfilling customer specific B2B requirements at TPM Flow level on demand and in time
  2. Reducing time to delivery and ensuring accelerated delivery in B2B migrations/implementations by using scalable generic packages, which can be used across all customers
  3. Close linkage to the product development and opportunity to influence and shape the product based on new demands from the customers in time
  4. Operational excellence by using B2B Monitoring which helps to monitor the processing interchanges with the different trading partners and is a crucial point for the company's business
  5. Staying up to date with new product features being released 

B2B Integration Factory - Some already available extensions

Looking at the B2B Integration Roadmap, the red marked features are already supported by the B2B Integration Factory:

Teaser

Additionally, we are also developing further extensions requested by customers.
Just to name one extension, it is the so called 'Parking of Messages'. 
Let's say the SAP ERP system is down over the weekend. All messages sent to the SAP ERP system should be parked in order to minimize traffic. Once the SAP ERP system is up and running again, the messages should be processed. 

Please drop a mail and tell us about the particular B2B requirements that are vital to your business.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 27th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 30th.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be Techtoberfest - Use this opportunity to show off your coding setup. This could be your desk, office, or even your favorite development tools.

List of activities for contest week 2: 

DAYBADGE CODECONTENTPOINTS
Monday   
Monday#AEC2D3Devtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 2, Sept. 301200
Monday#9AD6C0Get to Know SAP BTP, ABAP Environment150
Monday#6E4046Create an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment450
Monday#A28668Enhance an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) ABAP Environment600
Monday#5D5859Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object300
Monday#7662BEDevtoberfest 2024 - CAP, Week 2, Sept. 301200
Monday#E1B446Introduction to Application Development Using CAP70
Monday#D8EAFESet Up SAP Business Application Studio200
Monday#010101Build a CAP Application300
Monday#FAFAFAAdd SAP Fiori Elements UIs 300
Monday#E9E8F0Add Custom Logic100
Tuesday   
Tuesday#201926Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Week 2, Oct. 11200
Tuesday#99CEF1Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Watch DMS Video300
Tuesday#DC879BSetup SAP Build Code in SAP BTP100
Tuesday#212327Create a Full-Stack Application with SAP Fiori UI using Joule in SAP Build Code300
Tuesday#C3516FSubscribe to SAP Build Process Automation150
Tuesday#BA61B3Create a Business Process100
Tuesday#A8416DCreate and Configure Forms250
Tuesday#905335Create a Process Condition100
Tuesday#19E5E5Run the Business Process150
Wednesday   
Wednesday#1B573DDevtoberfest 2024 - Integration, Week 2, Oct. 21200
Wednesday#28B07ALearn about SAP Event Mesh100
Wednesday#B894A3Create Instance of SAP Event Mesh150
Wednesday#10302ACreate Queues and Queue Subscriptions for SAP Event Mesh300
Wednesday#030501Send and Receive Test SAP Event Mesh150
Wednesday#C6CAB4Create an Application for Producing Messages200
Wednesday#27302BCreate an Application for Receiving Messages200
Wednesday#62BAC4Create App for Sending and Receiving Messages Using a Java-Based Client450
Thursday   
Thursday#190005Devtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Week 2 - Oct. 32400
Thursday#FEF5F4Use Trial to Set Up Account for Data Attribute Recommendation and Download Postman Sample Files50
Thursday#2A2D30Install Postman REST Client50
Thursday#DAD8D6Set Up Postman to Call Data Attribute Recommendation APIs50
Thursday#FCEDEDUpload Data to Data Attribute Recommendation150
Thursday#D3C4D4Use Data Attribute Recommendation to Train a Machine Learning Model150
Friday   
Friday#53FA4CDevtoberfest 2024 - Frontend, Week 2, Oct. 041200
Friday More Activities being added soon. Please check back.
